children.text: Review by United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit; writ of certiorari to Supreme Court

Except as provided in subsection (b), the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit shall have exclusive jurisdiction to determine the validity of a final judgment rendered by a military commission (as approved by the convening authority and, where applicable, as affirmed or set aside as incorrect in law by the United States Court of Military Commission Review) under this chapter.

The United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit may not review a final judgment described in subsection (a) until all other appeals under this chapter have been waived or exhausted.
A petition for review by the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit must be filed in the Court of Appeals—
not later than 20 days after the date on which written notice of the final decision of the United States Court of Military Commission Review is served on the parties; or
if the accused submits, in the form prescribed by

children.tail: , a written notice waiving the right of the accused to review by the United States Court of Military Commission Review, not later than 20 days after the date on which such notice is submitted.

children.text: The United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit may act under this section only with respect to the findings and sentence as approved by the convening authority and as affirmed or set aside as incorrect in law by the United States Court of Military Commission Review, and shall take action only with respect to matters of law, including the sufficiency of the evidence to support the verdict.
The Supreme Court may review by writ of certiorari pursuant to

children.tail: the final judgment of the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit under this section.

children.text: Pub. L. 111–84, div. A, title XVIII, § 1802
Pub. L. 112–81, div. A, title X, § 1034(d)

children.tail: , related to review by the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit and the Supreme Court, prior to the general amendment of this chapter by
, inserted “as affirmed or set aside as incorrect in law by” after “where applicable,”.
, substituted “in the Court of Appeals—” for “by the accused in the Court of Appeals not later than 20 days after the date on which—” in introductory provisions.
, inserted “not later than 20 days after the date on which” before “written notice” and substituted “on the parties” for “on the accused or on defense counsel”.
, inserted “if” before “the accused submits” and inserted before period at end “, not later than 20 days after the date on which such notice is submitted”.
